types.name: ExtractionPattern

fields.name: capture_output
fields.doc: What to include in each capture result.

fields.name: child_fields
fields.doc:
Field names to extract from child nodes of each capture.
Maps a label to a tree-sitter field name used with `child_by_field_name`.

fields.name: max_results
fields.doc: Maximum number of matches to return. `None` means unlimited.

fields.name: byte_range
fields.doc: Restrict matches to a byte range `(start, end)`.

types.doc: Defines a single extraction pattern and its configuration.

types.name: ExtractionConfig

fields.name: patterns
fields.doc: Named patterns to run. Keys become the keys in `ExtractionResult::results`.

types.doc: Configuration for an extraction run against a single language.

types.name: CaptureResult

fields.name: name
fields.doc: The capture name from the query (e.g., `"fn_name"`).

fields.name: node
fields.doc: The `NodeInfo` snapshot, present when `CaptureOutput` is `Node` or `Full`.

fields.name: text
fields.doc: The matched source text, present when `CaptureOutput` is `Text` or `Full`.

fields.name: child_fields
fields.doc: Values of requested child fields, keyed by field name.

fields.name: start_byte
fields.doc: Byte offset where this capture starts in the source.

types.doc: A single captured node within a match.

types.name: MatchResult

fields.name: pattern_index
fields.doc: The pattern index within the query that produced this match.

types.doc: A single query match containing one or more captures.

types.name: PatternResult

fields.name: total_count
fields.doc: Total number of matches before `max_results` truncation.

types.doc: Results for a single named pattern.

types.name: ExtractionResult
types.doc: Complete extraction results for all patterns.

types.name: PatternValidation

fields.name: capture_names
fields.doc: Names of captures defined in the query.

fields.name: pattern_count
fields.doc: Number of patterns in the query.

types.doc: Validation information for a single pattern.

types.name: ValidationResult
types.doc: Validation results for an entire extraction config.

types.name: Span
types.doc:
Byte and line/column range in source code.

Represents both byte offsets (for slicing) and human-readable line/column
positions (for display and diagnostics).

types.name: ProcessResult

fields.name: extractions
fields.doc: Results of custom extraction patterns (when `config.extractions` is set).

types.doc:
Complete analysis result from processing a source file.

Contains metrics, structural analysis, imports/exports, comments,
docstrings, symbols, diagnostics, and optionally chunked code segments.
Fields are populated based on the [`crate::ProcessConfig`] flags.

# Fields

- `language` - The language used for parsing
- `metrics` - Always computed: line counts, byte sizes, error counts
- `structure` - Functions, classes, structs (when `config.structure = true`)
- `imports` - Import statements (when `config.imports = true`)
- `exports` - Export statements (when `config.exports = true`)
- `comments` - Comments (when `config.comments = true`)
- `docstrings` - Docstrings (when `config.docstrings = true`)
- `symbols` - Symbol definitions (when `config.symbols = true`)
- `diagnostics` - Parse errors (when `config.diagnostics = true`)
- `chunks` - Chunked code segments (when `config.chunk_max_size` is set)

types.name: FileMetrics
types.doc: Aggregate metrics for a source file.

types.name: StructureItem
types.doc: A structural item (function, class, struct, etc.) in source code.

types.name: CommentInfo
types.doc: A comment extracted from source code.

types.name: DocstringInfo
types.doc: A docstring extracted from source code.

types.name: DocSection
types.doc: A section within a docstring (e.g., Args, Returns, Raises).

types.name: ImportInfo
types.doc: An import statement extracted from source code.

types.name: ExportInfo
types.doc: An export statement extracted from source code.

types.name: SymbolInfo
types.doc: A symbol (variable, function, type, etc.) extracted from source code.

types.name: Diagnostic
types.doc: A diagnostic (syntax error, missing node, etc.) from parsing.

types.name: CodeChunk
types.doc: A chunk of source code with rich metadata.

types.name: ChunkContext
types.doc: Metadata for a single chunk of source code.

types.name: NodeInfo

fields.name: is_named
fields.doc: Whether this is a named node (vs anonymous like punctuation).

fields.name: is_error
fields.doc: Whether this node is an ERROR node.

fields.name: is_missing
fields.doc: Whether this node is a MISSING node.

types.doc:
Lightweight snapshot of a tree-sitter node's properties.

Contains only primitive types for easy cross-language serialization.
This is an owned type that can be passed across FFI boundaries, unlike
`tree_sitter::Node` which borrows from the tree.

types.name: PackConfig

fields.name: languages
fields.doc:
Languages to pre-download on init.

Each entry is a language name (e.g. `"python"`, `"rust"`).

types.name: ProcessConfig

fields.name: structure
fields.doc: Extract structural items (functions, classes, etc.). Default: true.

fields.name: chunk_max_size
fields.doc: Maximum chunk size in bytes. `None` disables chunking.

fields.name: extractions
fields.doc:
Custom extraction patterns to run against the parsed tree.
Keys become the keys in `ProcessResult::extractions`.

types.name: LanguageRegistry

methods.name: get_language
methods.doc:
Get a tree-sitter [`Language`] by name.

Resolves aliases (e.g., `"shell"` -> `"bash"`, `"makefile"` -> `"make"`),
then looks up the language in the static table. When the `dynamic-loading`
feature is enabled, falls back to loading a shared library on demand.

# Errors

Returns [`Error::LanguageNotFound`] if the name (after alias resolution)
does not match any known grammar.

methods.name: available_languages
methods.doc:
List all available language names, sorted and deduplicated.

Includes statically compiled languages, dynamically loadable languages
(if the `dynamic-loading` feature is enabled), and all configured aliases.

methods.name: has_language
methods.doc:
Check whether a language is available by name or alias.

Returns `true` if the language can be loaded, either from the static
table or from a dynamic library on disk.

methods.name: language_count
methods.doc: Return the total number of available languages (including aliases).

methods.name: process
methods.doc: Parse source code and extract file intelligence based on config in a single pass.

functions.name: detect_language_from_extension
detect_language_from_path
extension_ambiguity
detect_language_from_content
functions.doc:
Detect language name from file content using the shebang line (`#!`).

Inspects only the first line of `content`. If it begins with `#!`, the
interpreter name is extracted and mapped to a language name.

Handles common patterns:
- `#!/usr/bin/env python3` → `"python"`
- `#!/bin/bash` → `"bash"`
- `#!/usr/bin/env node` → `"javascript"`

The `-S` flag accepted by some `env` implementations is skipped automatically.
Version suffixes (e.g. `python3.11`, `ruby3.2`) are stripped before matching.

Returns `None` when content does not start with `#!`, the shebang is
malformed, or the interpreter is not recognised.

```
use tree_sitter_language_pack::detect_language_from_content;
assert_eq!(detect_language_from_content("#!/usr/bin/env python3\npass"), Some("python"));
assert_eq!(detect_language_from_content("#!/bin/bash\necho hi"), Some("bash"));
assert_eq!(detect_language_from_content("no shebang here"), None);
```

functions.name: process
functions.doc: Process source code: parse once, extract intelligence based on config, and return it.

functions.name: root_node_info
functions.doc: Get a `NodeInfo` snapshot of the root node.

functions.name: find_nodes_by_type
functions.doc:
Find all nodes matching the given type name, returning their `NodeInfo`.

Performs a depth-first traversal. Returns an empty vec if no matches.

functions.name: named_children_info
functions.doc:
Get `NodeInfo` for all named children of the root node.

Useful for understanding the top-level structure of a file
(e.g., list of function definitions, class declarations, imports).

functions.name: tree_contains_node_type
functions.doc:
Check whether any node in the tree matches the given type name.

Performs a depth-first traversal using `TreeCursor`.

functions.name: tree_has_error_nodes
functions.doc:
Check whether the tree contains any ERROR or MISSING nodes.

Useful for determining if the parse was clean or had syntax errors.

functions.name: tree_to_sexp
functions.doc:
Return the S-expression representation of the entire tree.

This is the standard tree-sitter debug format, useful for logging,
snapshot testing, and debugging grammars.

functions.name: tree_error_count
functions.doc:
Count the number of ERROR and MISSING nodes in the tree.

Returns 0 for a clean parse.

functions.name: get_highlights_query
get_injections_query
get_locals_query
split_code
functions.doc:
Split source code into chunks using tree-sitter AST structure for intelligent boundaries.
Returns a list of `(start_byte, end_byte)` ranges.

The algorithm works by:
1. Walking the tree-sitter AST to collect all nodes with their depth.
2. Using depth as a semantic level: shallower nodes (functions, classes) are
   preferred split boundaries over deeper nodes (statements, expressions).
3. Greedily merging adjacent sections at the best semantic level that keeps
   each chunk under `max_chunk_size` bytes.
4. When no AST node boundary fits, falling back to line boundaries and
   ultimately to raw byte splits.

The function never splits in the middle of a token/leaf node when an AST
boundary is available.

# Arguments

* `source` - The full source code string.
* `tree`   - A tree-sitter `Tree` previously parsed from `source`.
* `max_chunk_size` - Maximum size in bytes for each chunk.

# Returns

A `Vec<(usize, usize)>` of `(start_byte, end_byte)` ranges covering the
entire source. Ranges are non-overlapping, contiguous, and each range is
at most `max_chunk_size` bytes (except when a single indivisible token
exceeds that limit).

functions.name: available_languages
has_language
language_count

enums.name: Error
enums.doc:
Errors that can occur when using the tree-sitter language pack.

Covers language lookup failures, parse errors, query errors, and I/O issues.
Feature-gated variants are included when `config`, `download`, or related
features are enabled.

enums.name: CaptureOutput
enums.doc: Controls what data is captured for each query match.

enums.name: StructureKind
enums.doc:
The kind of structural item found in source code.

Categorizes top-level and nested declarations such as functions, classes,
structs, enums, traits, and more. Use [`Other`](StructureKind::Other) for
language-specific constructs that do not fit a standard category.

enums.name: CommentKind
enums.doc:
The kind of a comment found in source code.

Distinguishes between single-line comments, block (multi-line) comments,
and documentation comments.

enums.name: DocstringFormat
enums.doc:
The format of a docstring extracted from source code.

Identifies the docstring convention used, which varies by language
(e.g., Python triple-quoted strings, JSDoc, Rustdoc `///` comments).

enums.name: ExportKind
enums.doc:
The kind of an export statement found in source code.

Covers named exports, default exports, and re-exports from other modules.

enums.name: SymbolKind
enums.doc:
The kind of a symbol definition found in source code.

Categorizes symbol definitions such as variables, constants, functions,
classes, types, interfaces, enums, and modules.

enums.name: DiagnosticSeverity
enums.doc:
Severity level of a diagnostic produced during parsing.

Used to classify parse errors, warnings, and informational messages
found in the syntax tree.
